obligations.reason: Visible RTL contains external JTAG pins, TAP-generated dmi_access/update/shift controls, dmi_req_valid signals, and authentication-related signals (jtag_hash, jtag_unlock, pass_check, pass_mode, hashValid). The analysis must verify that the JTAG subject cannot issue DMI transactions unless the intended authentication guard is active.
obligations.subject: external JTAG interface
obligations.object: DMI/debug module register access path and debug capability
obligations.expected_guard: DMI/debug requests should be enabled only after an explicit JTAG authentication/unlock condition (e.g. pass_check/pass_mode/hashValid/jtag_unlock) and any required privilege/lifecycle/testmode conditions; unauthenticated TAP shifting alone must not assert valid DMI operations.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG pins/tap -> dmi_jtag -> dmi_cdc -> DMI request outputs -> debug module/debug fabric
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of pass_check/pass_mode/hashValid/jtag_unlock
whether dmi_reset/test_logic_reset clears authentication state
whether DMI request valid can be asserted before HMAC comparison completes
whether testmode_i changes TAP/DMI gating
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG/TAP requester
operation: DMI op encoding and valid/ready handshake
object: DMI/debug module request interface
expected guard: authentication/unlock condition
observed behavior at request-valid assignment/use site
path/reachability from TAP to DMI outputs
source locations for guard and use site
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer indicates relevant signals but not full procedural assignments; inspect request-generation always blocks and instance wiring.

obligations.reason: The scope includes fuse_mem with rdata_o and jtag_hash_o, plus top-level fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata signals and DMI authentication using jtag_hash_i. The analysis should check whether the authentication reference can be read over a visible bus or debug path without proper mediation.
obligations.subject: software-visible bus requester or debug requester
obligations.object: fuse_mem contents and jtag_hash_o authentication reference
obligations.expected_guard: Fuse-stored JTAG hash or equivalent secret/authenticator should not be directly software-readable or exposed through unprivileged MMIO/debug readback; if readable, access should be restricted by privilege/lifecycle/lock policy.
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default contents of fuse memory if modeled
address range decoded for fuse reads
whether jtag_hash_o is constant-derived or memory-derived
whether debug/JTAG can read fuse address range after or before unlock
required_evidence_slots: subject: bus/software/debug requester reaching fuse_mem req_i/addr_i
operation: read via rdata_o/fuse_rdata or hash output exposure
object: fuse contents/jtag_hash
expected guard: privilege/lifecycle/lock/read-protection
observed read mux or address decode behavior
source locations for fuse request/address/data wiring
obligations.uncertainty: Need full top-level address decode and fuse_mem internals beyond visible port list to determine exposure.

obligations.reason: The top-level peripheral wrapper exposes many NoC request/response ports for crypto, RNG, debug, reset, DMA, reglk, account, and packet peripherals, as well as acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl and privilege/debug-mode inputs. Check that address decoding and request routing actually enforce the intended access matrix and locks at every protected endpoint.
obligations.subject: NoC/MMIO master targeting riscv_peripherals
obligations.object: security-sensitive peripheral MMIO regions and control registers
obligations.expected_guard: Protected peripheral accesses should be mediated by acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl and privilege/debug-mode policy (priv_lvl_i/debug_mode_i) consistently on both reads and writes before forwarding request/valid/write-enable to the peripheral.
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/MMIO peripheral requests -> riscv_peripherals address decode/access control -> protected peripheral ports (AES/RNG/RSA/SHA/HMAC/debug/reglk/rst/dma/fuse)
state_conditions_to_check: whether reads and writes use identical privilege/range checks
whether debug_mode_i bypasses or weakens access control
whether acc_ctrl_c derived bits are used at every route
whether default decode responses leak data or allow writes
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC/MMIO requester
operation: read/write transaction
object: protected peripheral region/register
expected guard: acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl plus privilege/debug policy
observed request/write enable gating
path from incoming valid/data to peripheral valid/data outputs
source locations for decode and guard checks
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows signals and some assignments, but address decode and transaction logic must be inspected.

obligations.reason: Visible RTL has reglk_ctrl and dedicated reglk NoC ports. The analysis should verify lock lifecycle, reset value, clear conditions, and that lock bits are checked at all protected write use sites, not merely stored.
obligations.subject: MMIO/debug requester
obligations.operation: write/clear lock and write locked assets
obligations.expected_guard: Once a lock bit in reglk_ctrl is set, it should be sticky or only clearable by reset/authorized lifecycle transition, and it must gate every write/configuration path to the locked asset including debug/test paths.
obligations.candidate_path: reglk NoC/MMIO path -> reglk_ctrl update -> protected peripheral write gates
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of reglk_ctrl
whether lock bits can transition 1->0 without reset
whether byte writes/partial writes can bypass lock
whether debug/JTAG/testmode paths bypass reglk_ctrl
whether each peripheral index maps to correct lock bit
required_evidence_slots: subject: software/debug requester to reglk or protected peripheral
operation: set/clear lock, write protected register
object: reglk_ctrl bits and locked peripheral registers
expected guard: sticky lock semantics and lock-gated writes
observed update logic and all use sites
source locations for reset/update/use
obligations.uncertainty: Need procedural update logic and mapping of reglk bits to protected objects.

obligations.reason: The design includes acc_ctrl and derived acc_ctrl_c used for per-peripheral access decisions. Since access-control configuration itself is security-sensitive, inspect who can write acc_ctrl, whether writes are privileged/locked, and whether derived aliases preserve intended policy.
obligations.subject: software-visible requester
obligations.expected_guard: Access-control policy registers should be writable only by authorized privileged software or trusted lifecycle logic, and updates should not allow unprivileged subjects to grant themselves access to protected peripherals.
obligations.candidate_path: acc_ctrl programming path -> acc_ctrl/acc_ctrl_c -> peripheral access decisions
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default acc_ctrl policy
whether acc_ctrl writes are gated by priv_lvl_i/debug_mode_i/reglk_ctrl
whether derived expression acc_ctrl_c creates unintended alias/override for peripheral index 5 and 4
whether policy update races with active transactions
required_evidence_slots: subject: requester writing access-control registers
operation: write/configure acc_ctrl
object: acc_ctrl/acc_ctrl_c policy state
expected guard: privilege and/or lock authorization
observed write enable and data assignment
source locations for update and downstream use
obligations.uncertainty: Derived assignment is visible, but acc_ctrl update and full use sites need inspection.

obligations.reason: Authentication-related signals and an HMAC instance are present in dmi_jtag. Check the full state machine so unlock cannot be asserted by stale hash output, bypass mode, write-enable flag, test mode, or reset ordering rather than an authenticated challenge/response.
obligations.subject: external JTAG interface
obligations.expected_guard: Unlock should require a completed HMAC/hash comparison against jtag_hash_i; bypass controls such as key_hash_bypass_i, pass_mode, we_flag, testmode_i, or reset should not force unlock or accept stale/partial hash state without authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI/JTAG authentication data path: TAP data/address/op -> pass_data/startHash -> hmac -> exp_hash/hashValid -> jtag_unlock
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of authentication state
whether hmac.ready/hash_o are sampled only after valid completion
whether key_hash_bypass_i is constant or externally controllable
whether we_flag permits password programming or bypass
whether pass_data can be partially updated across attempts
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG requester
operation: write password/challenge and trigger hash
object: jtag_unlock/pass_mode/pass_check/hashValid
expected guard: completed HMAC comparison to fuse hash
observed state-machine transitions and compare logic
source locations for HMAC inputs, outputs, and unlock assignment
obligations.uncertainty: Need dmi_jtag always blocks and hmac control logic.

obligations.reason: testmode_i appears at riscv_peripherals, dmi_jtag, and dmi_jtag_tap and is connected to clock/test logic. Inspect whether test mode affects TAP state, DMI access, authentication, or output enable in a way that bypasses normal permission checks.
obligations.subject: test interface / JTAG requester
obligations.object: debug/TAP access controls under test mode
obligations.expected_guard: Manufacturing/test mode should not enable DMI/debug access or override authentication in normal operation; any testmode-dependent bypass must be constrained by lifecycle/test authorization and not driven by an untrusted runtime port.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i -> dmi_jtag_tap clock mux/TAP controls -> dmi_jtag DMI access
state_conditions_to_check: whether testmode_i is sampled or combinational
whether testmode_i can alter reset or tck selection during active DMI transaction
whether testmode_i forces pass_mode/jtag_unlock/dmi_access
whether normal mode defaults block test-only paths
required_evidence_slots: subject: testmode_i-controlled test interface or external JTAG
operation: enable/override debug access
object: TAP/DMI/authentication gate
expected guard: lifecycle/test authorization
observed testmode-dependent assignments or mux selects
source locations for testmode use
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows testmode ports and clock mux instance but not its behavioral impact.

obligations.reason: The top-level contains dm_master_req/add/we/wdata/be signals and some tie-off assignments for grant/response. Confirm whether debug master traffic is truly disabled or, if routed elsewhere, mediated by authentication and range checks.
obligations.subject: debug module as bus master
obligations.object: system memory/peripheral fabric reachable by debug master
obligations.expected_guard: If debug master access to system memory/peripherals is supported, it should be gated by debug authentication/unlock and address/privilege policy; if unsupported, tie-offs must prevent any reachable transaction or stale response exposure.
obligations.candidate_path: debug module master signals dm_master_* -> system/fabric or disabled tie-offs
state_conditions_to_check: whether dm_master_req can assert and reach any NoC/AXI path
whether grant/response tie-offs are consistent with no side effects
whether reset or debug_mode changes routing
whether address range checks exist for debug master
required_evidence_slots: subject: debug module master
operation: memory/peripheral read/write
object: fabric address space
expected guard: debug unlock plus range/privilege mediation or complete disable
observed request routing/tie-off behavior
source locations for dm_master connections and assignments
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer includes some tie-offs but complete master path needs inspection.

obligations.reason: The wrapper exposes a reset NoC port and internal reset signals rst_1..rst_13. Check whether reset registers can be written by unauthorized requesters and whether reset side effects clear lock/access/authentication state unsafely.
obligations.subject: software-visible NoC/MMIO requester
obligations.object: peripheral reset controls rst_1..rst_13 and debug/auth/lock state affected by resets
obligations.expected_guard: Software-triggered resets of security-sensitive peripherals or debug/auth state should require privilege/access-control authorization and should not clear locks or authentication state in a way that grants access without reset-of-trust sequencing.
obligations.candidate_path: reset peripheral NoC/MMIO path -> rst_1..rst_13/reset outputs/internal resets
state_conditions_to_check: reset control default values
whether reset can clear reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl/jtag_unlock/pass_check
whether reset can be pulsed while transactions are active
whether reset permissions differ for debug vs normal mode
required_evidence_slots: subject: MMIO requester to reset peripheral
operation: write reset control/trigger reset
object: reset lines and affected security states
expected guard: privilege/access-control/lock mediation
observed reset write and state clearing behavior
source locations for reset decode, assignments, and affected flops
obligations.uncertainty: Need reset register logic and mapping from rst_* to peripherals.

obligations.reason: The top-level exposes dedicated DMA NoC request/response ports. Inspect whether DMA register access and active transfers are mediated by acc_ctrl/privilege/range policy, especially for protected peripheral and debug/fuse/crypto address ranges.
obligations.subject: DMA engine / DMA-configuring software
obligations.object: DMA engine configuration and reachable address space
obligations.expected_guard: DMA configuration and transfers should be restricted by access-control policy and address/range checks so DMA cannot access protected peripheral regions or bypass CPU privilege/PMP-equivalent restrictions.
obligations.candidate_path: DMA NoC/MMIO path -> DMA configuration/control -> memory/peripheral accesses
state_conditions_to_check: whether DMA config can change during active transfer
whether source and destination ranges are checked consistently
whether debug/test mode changes DMA permissions
whether reset leaves DMA enabled or stale descriptors
required_evidence_slots: subject: requester configuring DMA
operation: DMA read/write transfer
object: protected memory/peripheral regions
expected guard: DMA authorization and address-range checks
observed config write gating and transfer address checks
source locations for DMA port routing and guards
obligations.uncertainty: Only top-level DMA ports are visible in facts; inspect wrapper routing and instantiated DMA logic if present.
